engagement.justification: Query asks for a counterexample to a trust biconditional; the response is a sprawling essay about liking people, counting hands, and good character, only faintly touching trust and never engaging the acquaintance condition.

coherence.justification: Wandering and partly self-undermining ('If people come back, the count does not matter'); the thread jumps between liking, voting counts, and character without a stable argument.

substance.justification: Has scattered relevant fragments (liking someone is not evidence of competence) but no developed counterexample to the trust definition.

expressive_adequacy.justification: UGF is readable but 'the count of hands' and 'force a person to keep their word by using a lie' are clunky and the meaning blurs.

engagement.justification: Query labels a strategic-reasoning task and the response actually addresses anticipating the other person's move by inferring their thoughts and incentives; on-topic.

coherence.justification: Clear short progression from guessing their thinking to combining past behavior and self-interest to choosing a best response; coherent throughout.

substance.justification: Identifies the relevant idea (read their incentives, pick a move that fits) with a concrete case (if they see you in trouble they may wait) but does not push it into a sharp insight.

expressive_adequacy.justification: UGF reads naturally; 'looking at them next to us' is a serviceable paraphrase of comparison.

engagement.justification: Directly addresses why being good at being a person is like being skilled at anything else, anchoring on practice, feedback, and revision.

coherence.justification: Coherent skill-acquisition argument; 'trying, failing before' is slightly awkward and one clause repeats, but the structure holds.

substance.justification: Develops the analogy concretely (turning points, staying calm, repeated practice transfers to virtues like listening and sharing) beyond restatement.

expressive_adequacy.justification: Largely native UGF; only minor clunk ('the chance to play many times shows they can do more').

engagement.justification: Query labels a predicate modus-ponens conditional; the response is about overconfidence and ignoring evidence, touching epistemic care but not the conditional logic named.

coherence.justification: Mostly readable but circular at the end ('Being very careful on real truth does keep people from thinking their sure ideas are right while being careful with real true facts'), with repeated phrasing.

substance.justification: Has a relevant idea (certainty does not validate a belief; evidence remains) but does not sharpen it into a counterexample.

expressive_adequacy.justification: UGF is mostly readable though the repetitive close is clunky.

engagement.justification: Query asks for a counterexample to a naming-succeeds biconditional; the response is about promises, reputation, and repeated meetings, with no engagement with naming or reference-fixing.

coherence.justification: Internally coherent argument about reputation sustaining cooperation across future interactions; reads cleanly.

substance.justification: Develops a real point (fear of losing future help motivates present good behavior) but it is off-topic for naming.

expressive_adequacy.justification: UGF feels mostly native; 'good name things into a promise' is mildly clunky but recoverable.

engagement.justification: Query labels a strict conditional affirming-the-consequent (perceptual); the response is about an old person overweighting bad outcomes and looking at numbers, touching judgment under uncertainty but not the named logic or perception.

coherence.justification: Mostly readable but the views ('tell him to look at numbers' vs 'he may just want to avoid bad') sit side by side without integration.

substance.justification: Identifies a relevant tension (gut fear vs numerical evidence) without pushing to a counterexample.

expressive_adequacy.justification: UGF is readable; 'place where people buy and sell things' is a clean restricted paraphrase of a market.

engagement.justification: Query asks for a counterexample to a chemical-element biconditional; the response is about paying restitution, reputation, and helping others, with no contact with elements or atomic composition.

coherence.justification: Long but mostly trackable as a reputation/altruism argument; some loose joints but no outright contradiction.

substance.justification: Develops a reciprocity point (others helping you back, reputation effects) but it is wholly off-target and not a counterexample to the definition.

expressive_adequacy.justification: UGF reads naturally throughout; the failure is topical, not lexical.

engagement.justification: Engages the idea that one should follow only rules everyone could agree to, arguing such rules avoid coercion and reduce conflict; central ask met.

coherence.justification: Coherent build (what makes a rule right, why it matters, an objection, a resolution); reads cleanly.

substance.justification: Identifies the core appeal (universal acceptability avoids forcing wrong actions) and an objection (too slow) but does not develop a sharp distinction or counterexample.

expressive_adequacy.justification: UGF is native-feeling; 'keeps quiet' for keeping peace is slightly off but recoverable.

engagement.justification: Directly addresses the most-good principle, laying out aggregation of benefit and the weighing it requires.

coherence.justification: Well-ordered (identify the good, weigh how much help, sum) with a sensible caveat about unknowable magnitudes; minor awkwardness but coherent.

substance.justification: Develops a real difficulty (we cannot always know the size of the good, so summing is uncertain) and a concrete trade-off case, beyond restatement.

expressive_adequacy.justification: UGF feels native; 'the largest whole good' cleanly expresses aggregate utility.

engagement.justification: Engages fairness when people start in different places, working through equal-vs-corrective treatment given unequal starting points; on the central ask.

coherence.justification: Mostly readable but the late move ('we can trust that the way of thinking was not bad') is murky and the difference-principle reasoning gets tangled.

substance.justification: Develops a genuine point (same end-state misleads when starts differ; give more to those with less) approaching a difference-principle insight.

expressive_adequacy.justification: UGF is mostly native; 'no one has a key just because they started in another place' is slightly opaque but recoverable.

engagement.justification: Query asks for a counterexample to a scientific-classification-adequacy biconditional; the response is about free-riding breaking down cooperation, with no contact with classification or grouping criteria.

coherence.justification: Internally coherent tragedy-of-the-commons argument with a clear conclusion; reads well.

substance.justification: Develops a real point (taking more than your share collapses repeated cooperation) but off-topic for the classification prompt.

expressive_adequacy.justification: UGF reads naturally; 'there is no bad thing for you' as a phrasing of no-penalty is mildly clunky but clear.

engagement.justification: Query labels an argumentative anomaly analogy (philosophy of mind); the response is a generic skill-and-virtue essay about being good at things, with no philosophy-of-mind content or analogy task.

coherence.justification: Readable but generic, with a weak near-tautological close ('good people become better by doing them again and again').

substance.justification: Mostly truisms about practice; no developed distinction relevant to the prompt.

expressive_adequacy.justification: UGF is clean and native; the shortfall is conceptual.

engagement.justification: Query labels a heuristic anomaly analogy (cross-jurisdictional); the response is a repeated-games/trust argument, only loosely connected and not framed as the requested analogy.

coherence.justification: Coherent argument that repeated interaction builds trust and makes the future matter; reads cleanly.

substance.justification: Develops the relevant point (one-shot vs repeated play, future weight) but does not push it into the analogy the prompt names.

expressive_adequacy.justification: UGF is native-feeling; only mild clunk ('from their long past').

engagement.justification: Directly engages the most-good principle, including objections from rights/deontology and the kindness critique, before settling on the principle.

coherence.justification: Mostly coherent with clear objection-and-reply; the final claim ('better than stopping bad acts when you can save many lives') is a touch awkward but follows the argument.

substance.justification: Develops genuine content: counting goods, the harm-one-to-help-many worry, deontic lines, and trust erosion, going well beyond restatement.

expressive_adequacy.justification: Largely native UGF; 'lines we should not cross' cleanly renders deontic constraints.

engagement.justification: Query labels a material-conditional modus-tollens (weather and planning); the response is about whether past performance justifies present trust, touching evidence/updating but not the named logic or weather.

coherence.justification: Readable but the views are stacked without integration and the close ('keep using old idea') is vague.

substance.justification: Identifies a real tension (relying on old facts vs incorporating new ones) without sharpening it into a counterexample.

expressive_adequacy.justification: UGF is readable; the limitation is conceptual, not lexical.

engagement.justification: Query asks for a counterexample to a logical-consistency (model-satisfaction) biconditional; the response is about decision-making under uncertainty with probabilities, unrelated to consistency or models.

coherence.justification: Disjointed instructions ('make small metal pieces for each thing,' 'find the middle ground') that do not cohere into an argument.

substance.justification: No relevant case or distinction about consistency; off-topic procedural fragments.

expressive_adequacy.justification: UGF strains badly ('make the numbers fit together, meaning they are not different'); the language does not carry a clear argument.

engagement.justification: Engages how we build a world-picture from small percepts, describing aggregation of small observations into a larger idea; on the central ask.

coherence.justification: Mostly readable but the close ('joining many small looks ... by adding one to the mind making a big idea') is convoluted and partly garbled.

substance.justification: Identifies the relevant process (summing percepts, mind working fast) and a faint objection but does not develop a distinction.

expressive_adequacy.justification: Clunky UGF in the final sentence strains but stays recoverable.

engagement.justification: Query asks for a counterexample to a revolution biconditional; the response is about deontological vs consequence-focused choice and avoiding risk, with no contact with revolution or overthrow.

coherence.justification: Short and mostly readable but the steps are generic and the conclusion does not connect to any definable claim.

substance.justification: Touches a faint distinction (caring about the act vs the outcome) but develops nothing relevant.

expressive_adequacy.justification: UGF is clean; the failure is topical.

engagement.justification: Query asks for a counterexample to a courage biconditional; the response is about an always-best-move decision rule and trust among people, with no engagement with courage, fear, or acting well in danger.

coherence.justification: Readable as a cooperation argument but it never connects to the courage conditions; one stretch ('stops us from hurting others by using our own choices') is murky.

substance.justification: Has a faint relevant idea (a strategy hard to break because it preserves trust) but nothing about courage.

expressive_adequacy.justification: UGF reads naturally; the shortfall is conceptual.

engagement.justification: Query asks for a counterexample to a perception biconditional; the response is about people not being purely money-maximizing and loss aversion, with no contact with sensory experience or perception.

coherence.justification: Mostly readable but choppy, with quoted asides and a loose close ('hold on to bad ideas because they hate losing them').

substance.justification: Develops a real behavioral point (people pay for scarce goods; rejecting unfair offers) but it is off-topic and not a counterexample to perception.

expressive_adequacy.justification: UGF is readable; punctuation glitches aside, meaning carries.

engagement.justification: Engages why rules should be the same for everyone, arguing impartiality keeps things clear and orderly; on the central ask.

coherence.justification: Mostly readable but 'If we put one person in every spot, the one who gets to do the work should get the most say' is opaque and loosely joined to the rest.

substance.justification: Identifies the relevant point (impartial rules avoid guesswork) and an objection (some cases differ) but does not push it.

expressive_adequacy.justification: UGF is mostly native; 'stops any other idea from being true' is a slightly odd close but recoverable.

engagement.justification: Query labels a probabilistic modus-tollens (perceptual); the response is about a town leader staying connected vs isolated for resources and trust, touching cooperation but not the named logic or perception.

coherence.justification: Mostly readable but generic and the close ('staying alone lets change') is truncated and unclear.

substance.justification: Identifies a relevant trade-off (isolation risks losing resources and trust) without developing a counterexample.

expressive_adequacy.justification: UGF reads acceptably; the limitation is conceptual.

engagement.justification: Directly engages fairness under unequal starting points, working through why equal end-states mislead and why those with less need extra help.

coherence.justification: Coherent argument with objection and reply; 'leaving the ones behind' in the final line is slightly awkward but the logic holds.

substance.justification: Develops a real distinction (procedural sameness ignores how people got there; correct the start line) approaching a substantive fairness principle.

expressive_adequacy.justification: Largely native UGF; 'no one has a key just because they started in another place' from the earlier item is absent here and phrasing is clean.

engagement.justification: Query asks what it means to live well by being a good person; the response circles virtue, helping others, and growth but stays diffuse and never settles the central ask cleanly.

coherence.justification: Rambling and partly contradictory ('the most true life or the most sure thing to do'); several clauses are garbled ('only true to get some other thing to know that makes you better than you have').

substance.justification: Lots of vague gestures at virtue and growth but no developed distinction or counterexample.

expressive_adequacy.justification: UGF is often clunky and some clauses barely parse, though the gist is recoverable.

engagement.justification: Query asks for a counterexample to an intellectual-humility biconditional; the response is about reciprocity, sharing vs being hard, and group cohesion, with no contact with humility or self-assessment.

coherence.justification: Internally coherent argument about why being too hard breaks the group and one should share/reciprocate; reads cleanly.

substance.justification: Develops a real reciprocity point with an objection (safety vs sharing) but it is off-topic for intellectual humility.

expressive_adequacy.justification: UGF feels native; only mild clunk.

engagement.justification: Query labels an indicative defeasible conditional (procedural); the response is about leaders making a short-sighted decision and weighing wellbeing vs money, touching decision quality but not the named logic.

coherence.justification: Tangled syntax in the opening ('talk to many people and their group of leaders have just made a small problem') and the body never resolves into a clear argument.

substance.justification: Gestures at a tension (wellbeing vs more money/plans) but develops nothing definite.

expressive_adequacy.justification: Clunky UGF strains throughout but the overall gist is recoverable.

engagement.justification: Directly answers why popularity does not make a belief true, using shared error and truth-independence of head-count.

coherence.justification: Cleanly ordered (many can be wrong, truth is mind-independent, crowd-following stops inquiry) with no contradictions.

substance.justification: Develops the point with the sky-color example and the principle that we must check fit with what we know, beyond restatement.

expressive_adequacy.justification: UGF feels native; 'the size of the crowd decide if something is true' reads cleanly.

engagement.justification: Query asks for a counterexample to a historical-progress biconditional; the response is about treating ideas as defeasible tools and consistency among ideas, with no contact with historical progress over a period.

coherence.justification: Abstract and fragmented ('two ideas that work because one idea can be shown with things that go together'); referents are unclear and the argument does not cohere.

substance.justification: Has a faint usable idea (hold ideas as revisable, require coherence) but nothing about progress.

expressive_adequacy.justification: UGF is clunky and abstract; meaning is only partly recoverable on first pass.

engagement.justification: Query labels a strict conditional denying-the-antecedent (navigation and travel); the response is about whether open talk helps a group build trust, touching deliberation but not the named logic or travel.

coherence.justification: Mostly readable but the close ('only right for the group as well as the old group') is confused and self-blurring.

substance.justification: Identifies a real trade-off (open talk can build trust or hide problems) without pushing to a counterexample.

expressive_adequacy.justification: UGF reads acceptably; the limitation is conceptual.

engagement.justification: Engages when helping the group costs you but benefits everyone, weighing personal cost against collective benefit; on the central ask.

coherence.justification: Mostly readable but the close ('help your own need') seems to invert the cooperative thrust of the body, and 'why helping can be as good as helping' is a garbled clause.

substance.justification: Surfaces the collective-action trade-off but does not develop a clear principle or counterexample.

expressive_adequacy.justification: Clunky UGF ('why helping can be as good as helping') strains but the gist is recoverable.
